title: Less School, Better Scores
alt_text: Data visualization: instructional hours vs PISA scores — Finland low hours, top outcomes; US high hours, middling outcomes
slides:
Finland: 593 hours of school per year.
The US: 1,016 hours.

Finland scores near the top on PISA. The US scores below average for its wealth level.

Poland: 635 hours, PISA 516.
US: 1,016 hours, PISA 497.

More hours. Worse outcomes. Hours are not the constraint.

Finland accepts fewer than 10% of teacher training applicants. Teaching is harder to get into than medicine there.

The curriculum was redesigned around depth, not coverage. Hours were cut.

The US did the opposite.
sources: OECD Education at a Glance 2023 · PISA 2022
